src/-private/utils.ts.summary: Internal shared helper utilities used across Maybe, Result, and Task modules: void checks, currying, safe stringification, identity, and a bottom-function type.
src/-private/utils.ts.inputs: Arbitrary unknown values, unary operations plus optional item for currying, values needing string conversion.
src/-private/utils.ts.outputs: Boolean/type guards (isVoid), curried-or-applied results (curry1), strings (safeToString), the value itself (identity), the AnyFunction type.
src/-private/utils.ts.should: Provide tiny reusable primitives; treat null and undefined as void; fall back to JSON.stringify when no usable toString exists.
src/-private/utils.ts.shouldNot: Depend on any sibling module; expose domain types (Maybe/Result/Task); perform IO or async work.

src/index.ts.summary: Root barrel entry point re-exporting Maybe, Result, Task, Unit, toolbelt, and standard-schema as both namespaces and default classes for namespace-style consumption.
src/index.ts.inputs: None; only re-exports from sibling modules.
src/index.ts.outputs: Named namespace exports (maybe, result, task, toolbelt, standardSchema) and default exports (Maybe, Result, Task, Unit).
src/index.ts.should: Aggregate the public surface; keep both namespace and default forms; stay a pure re-export.
src/index.ts.shouldNot: Contain logic, types, or implementation; import internal -private helpers directly.

src/maybe.ts.summary: Defines the Maybe<T> type (Just/Nothing) with class implementation plus a full standalone/curried function API for optionality: map, andThen, unwrapOr, match, ap, find, get, transposeArray, etc.
src/maybe.ts.inputs: Values to wrap, mapping/predicate/matcher functions, arrays and objects, other Maybe instances, JSON representations.
src/maybe.ts.outputs: Maybe instances, unwrapped values, booleans, strings, JSON, and type guards.
src/maybe.ts.should: Treat null/undefined as Nothing; share one Nothing singleton; throw on just(null) and Nothing.value; mirror method and free-function forms.
src/maybe.ts.shouldNot: Import Result or Task (avoid coupling); mutate wrapped values; leak internal Impl class.

src/result.ts.summary: Defines the Result<T,E> type (Ok/Err) with class implementation plus standalone/curried function API for fallible sync operations: map, mapErr, andThen, unwrapOr, match, ap, tryOr, all, transposeAll, flatten.
src/result.ts.inputs: Success/error values, mapping and matcher functions, callbacks that may throw, arrays of Results, JSON representations.
src/result.ts.outputs: Result instances, unwrapped values, booleans, strings, JSON, type guards.
src/result.ts.should: Wrap thrown errors via tryOr/tryOrElse/safe; support Unit for empty ok()/err(); provide method and free-function parity.
src/result.ts.shouldNot: Import Maybe or Task; perform async work; throw from normal accessors on the matching variant.

src/standard-schema.ts.summary: Integrates Standard Schema-compliant validators (Zod, Arktype, Valibot) with True Myth, producing parsers that emit Result (sync) or Task (async) instead of throwing.
src/standard-schema.ts.inputs: A StandardSchemaV1 schema and unknown data to validate.
src/standard-schema.ts.outputs: parserFor -> Result<T, FailureResult>; asyncParserFor -> Task<T, FailureResult>; the StandardSchemaV1 interface/namespace types.
src/standard-schema.ts.should: Return Err/Rejected on validation issues; throw InvalidAsyncSchema when a sync parser gets a Promise; detect success by absence of issues.
src/standard-schema.ts.shouldNot: Bundle any specific schema library; assume presence of a value field alone means success.

src/task.ts.summary: Defines the Task<T,E> type (Pending/Resolved/Rejected), a PromiseLike wrapper over Result for async operations that never throw, with combinators: map, andThen, or, match, all, allSettled, any, race, timeout, withRetries.
src/task.ts.inputs: Executor callbacks, Promises, Results, other Tasks, retry delay strategies, timers/durations, matcher functions.
src/task.ts.outputs: Task instances, Promises of Result, aggregate/timeout rejection errors, type guards, retry helpers.
src/task.ts.should: Convert rejections into Rejected instead of throwing; lift Promises/Results into Tasks; track internal Pending/Resolved/Rejected state.
src/task.ts.shouldNot: Leak raw Promise rejections; throw on normal await; block the event loop.

src/task/delay.ts.summary: Retry-delay strategy generators for Task.withRetries: exponential, fibonacci, fixed, immediate, linear, none, plus a jitter helper. A Strategy is an Iterator<number>.
src/task/delay.ts.inputs: Optional config objects (from, withFactor, withStepSize, at) and a number for jitter.
src/task/delay.ts.outputs: Infinite Generator<number> yielding millisecond delays capped at MAX_SAFE_INTEGER; jitter returns a randomized number.
src/task/delay.ts.should: Round non-integral bases via Math.round; cap growth at Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER; keep jitter within 0..2n.
src/task/delay.ts.shouldNot: Perform the actual waiting or retrying; import Task or perform IO.

src/test-support.ts.summary: Test-only helpers to bluntly unwrap Maybe and Result values, throwing on the wrong variant, for concise assertions.
src/test-support.ts.outputs: unwrap -> the Just/Ok value; unwrapErr -> the Err error.
src/test-support.ts.should: Distinguish Maybe from Result via isInstance; throw when accessing the absent/wrong variant; stay thin.
src/test-support.ts.shouldNot: Be used in production paths; add safe fallback behavior; import Task.

src/toolbelt.ts.summary: Cross-conversion utilities between Maybe and Result kept separate so importing either core module need not pull in the other: toMaybe, fromMaybe, fromResult, toOkOrErr, toOkOrElseErr, transposeResult, transposeMaybe.
src/toolbelt.ts.inputs: Maybe or Result instances, error values or error-producing functions.
src/toolbelt.ts.outputs: Converted Maybe or Result instances, including transposed nested forms.
src/toolbelt.ts.should: Require both Maybe and Result; discard Err/error when narrowing to Maybe; support curried forms via curry1.
src/toolbelt.ts.shouldNot: Live inside maybe.ts or result.ts; import Task; mutate inputs.

src/unit.ts.summary: Provides Unit, a type-safe, non-interchangeable empty-object stand-in for null/undefined, equivalent to () in functional languages.
src/unit.ts.outputs: The Unit value (Object.create(null) branded via a unique symbol) and the Unit type/interface.
src/unit.ts.should: Be a single concrete safe instance; use nominal branding to stay distinct from other empties.
src/unit.ts.shouldNot: Carry data or methods; be confused with null/undefined; depend on other modules.
